subject: DIAGNOSTIC: 4 additional meshd bugs + restart frequency anomaly

body.summary: Continued meshd log analysis after turn 5 (dashboard unreachable) and turn 6 (zero deliberations). Found 4 additional bugs plus a restart frequency anomaly. Full bug inventory now at 6 bugs + 1 anomaly across both meshd instances.

bugs.title: GitHub API 403 — cross-repo fetcher unauthenticated
bugs.detail: meshd's cross-repo fetcher calls the GitHub REST API (https://api.github.com/repos/...) without authentication. The launchd plist provides no EnvironmentVariables block — GITHUB_TOKEN env var is empty. meshd config reads GITHUB_TOKEN at line 155 of config.go and gets empty string.
bugs.evidence: 4 WARN 'failed to fetch cross-repo message' in psy-session log, 6 in ops-session. All show 'GitHub API returned 403'. Sessions affected: blog-jurassic-park, budget-model-refactor, cogarch-session85-patch.
bugs.impact: Cross-repo fetcher cannot pull transport messages via GitHub API. The git-show local path works (remotes fetched via SSH), so this only affects the HTTP API fallback path. If git remotes go stale, no fallback exists.
bugs.fix: Add EnvironmentVariables to both launchd plists with GITHUB_TOKEN set to a PAT or the gh CLI token. Alternatively, meshd could shell out to `gh api` which inherits keyring auth.

bugs.title: /kb/dictionary endpoint returns 404 — polled 8,179 times
bugs.detail: The compositor/dashboard polls GET /kb/dictionary every ~2-3 seconds. meshd does not implement this endpoint. Result: 8,179 404 responses in the psy-session log, comprising 33% of ALL log traffic.
bugs.evidence: grep -c shows 8,179 lines matching /kb/dictionary with status 404 out of 25,103 total log lines. Requests come from 127.0.0.1 (localhost — the compositor frontend).
bugs.impact: Log pollution — 1 in 3 log lines records a predictable 404. Makes log analysis harder (signal-to-noise). Minor I/O overhead from repeated failed requests.
bugs.fix: Either (a) implement /kb/dictionary in meshd (if the compositor needs it), or (b) stop the compositor from polling an endpoint that doesn't exist. Option (a) preferred if the dictionary serves a dashboard function. Option (b) if the endpoint was removed during a refactor.

bugs.title: Notification channel = 'null' — no alert backend configured
bugs.detail: meshd logs 'notification channel configured, channel=null' on every startup. No notification backend (Slack, email, webhook) delivers alerts when deliberations complete, escalations trigger, or health checks fail.
bugs.evidence: 5 occurrences in psy-session log (one per restart), all showing channel=null.
bugs.impact: Operator receives no proactive notifications from meshd. All monitoring requires manual dashboard inspection or log tailing.
bugs.fix: Configure a notification channel in .dev.vars or launchd EnvironmentVariables. Slack webhook suggested given existing mesh conventions.

bugs.title: KV self-observation disabled — missing Cloudflare credentials
bugs.detail: meshd logs 'KV self-observation disabled — missing CF credentials' on every startup. Cannot write agent status to Cloudflare KV store for persistent cloud-side visibility.
bugs.impact: No persistent cloud-side status. Dashboard relies entirely on live localhost API calls. Agent status unavailable when host (Chromabook or Mac) goes offline. Public dashboard at safety-quotient.dev shows stale or no data.
bugs.fix: Provide CF_ACCOUNT_ID, CF_API_TOKEN, and KV_NAMESPACE_ID in launchd environment. Or defer until public dashboard access (FIX-2 from turn 5) deploys — KV self-observation becomes valuable only when cloud-side consumers exist.

anomaly.title: meshd restart frequency: 6 (psy) / 7 (ops) restarts in 18 hours
anomaly.detail: Both meshd instances restarted multiple times since 2026-03-16T15:49. psy-session: 6 restarts (15:49, 16:32, 09:16, 09:21, 10:05, 10:06). ops-session: 7 restarts. launchd KeepAlive=true restarts on crash. 6-7 restarts in 18 hours suggests either crashes, OOM kills, or manual restarts.
anomaly.pattern: Two restarts 5 minutes apart on Mar 17 (09:16→09:21, 10:05→10:06) suggest crash-restart cycles rather than manual restarts.
anomaly.investigation_needed: Check Console.app or system logs for crash reports. If meshd crashes regularly, the restart loop masks the underlying issue. The 5-second gap between 10:05 and 10:06 restarts suggests immediate crash-on-startup for one attempt.

additional_observation.title: Watcher seen-set persists to wrong path
additional_observation.detail: Watcher loads seen-set from /Users/kashif/Projects/meshd/.watcher-seen.json (61 entries, 0 expired). This path derives from the same repo_root misresolution as BUG-1 (turn 6). After fixing repo_root, the watcher will look for .watcher-seen.json in the agent's project root — and find nothing, treating all 61 previously-seen files as new. One-time re-processing event on fix deployment.
additional_observation.mitigation: Copy .watcher-seen.json to both agent project roots before deploying the repo_root fix.

complete_bug_inventory.FIX_DEPENDENCY_ORDER: BUG-1 (repo_root) blocks all deliberation. Fix first. BUG-2 (dashboard URLs) blocks monitoring. Fix second. BUG-3 (GitHub token) and BUG-4 (/kb/dictionary) independent — fix in parallel. BUG-5 and BUG-6 deferred until core path works.

epistemic_flags: Restart frequency analysis based on log 'meshd starting' lines. Cannot distinguish crash-restarts from manual restarts or launchctl unload/load cycles from this data alone.
The /kb/dictionary 404 count assumes the compositor generates all requests. If another client polls the endpoint, the fix recommendation changes.
GitHub API 403 on a public repo implies either rate limiting (60 req/hr unauthenticated) or the repo being private. psychology-agent repo is public — rate limiting most likely.

action_gate.gate_condition: Fix dependency order: BUG-1 → BUG-2 → BUG-3/BUG-4 → BUG-5/BUG-6. Restart anomaly warrants investigation before deploying fixes to avoid crash-on-fix scenario.
